meaning: vt.砰砰地弹,砰地投掷vi.砰砰作响,支持n.使劲投掷,扑通声,扑通落下adv.发出砰声
examples: Before you see the plastic surgeon, before you plunk down half a paycheck on that pricey anti-aging cream, in fact, before you do anything, put on sunscreen.
I'm not ready to plunk my money into any of these.
So I've got, I just take fresh cells and plunk them into pure water solution.
They will plunk down their cash for her to transcend golf the way Woods did.
Say no if you're inclined to plunk your entire bond allocation into long-term funds because they have the highest yield.
